text: In this environment, surrounded by the buzz of conversation and the sterile yet modern interiors, I feel a peculiar blend of anticipation and disconnection. The glass wall of the meeting room is both a barrier and a window—I observe and am observed. Moving to the exhibit, the words onscreen mirror my own uncertainty. I feel exposed, unsure if I am being truly understood here. There is a sense of longing for clarity and authentic connection, a desire for the visible and invisible layers of intention to harmonize. Despite the innovation and knowledge on display, I am reminded of the persistent complexities of human perception and the occasional loneliness that accompanies it.
